Applicability of Appointment Requirements, , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–293, title VIII, § 813(b)110 Stat. 3483Oct. 11, 1996“The requirement established by section 20 of the Central Intelligence Agency Act of 1949 [], as added by subsection (a), for the appointment by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, of an individual to the position of General Counsel of the Central Intelligence Agency shall apply as follows:50 U.S.C. 3520“(1)To any vacancy in such position that occurs after the date of the enactment of this Act [].Oct. 11, 1996“(2)To the incumbent serving in such position on the date of the enactment of this Act as of the date that is six months after such date of enactment, if such incumbent has served in such position continuously between such date of enactment and the date that is six months after such date of enactment.”

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: a Appointment
children.text: There is a General Counsel of the Central Intelligence Agency, appointed from civilian life by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate.

children.heading: Chief legal officer
children.label: b Chief legal officer
children.text: The General Counsel is the chief legal officer of the Central Intelligence Agency.

children.heading: Functions
children.label: c Functions
children.text: The General Counsel of the Central Intelligence Agency shall perform such functions as the Director may prescribe.
